source_jobs.title: Creative Operations Intern
source_jobs.description:
Creative Operations Intern Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia Corporation is a design, fashion and creative agency with studios in California and Seoul. We produce fashion, conceptual marketing campaigns, activations, events and cultural projects—often across all of them at once. We collaborate with underground artists, experimental musicians in both US and Asia frequently.

We're looking for an operations intern to help keep creative projects organized and moving—across both physical and digital production. You'll support shoots, events, campaigns, and digital projects from planning through delivery—tracking timelines, coordinating workflows, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.

You:

 - Current student or recent graduate in a relevant field, or 0–2 years in production, operations, or project coordination

 - Highly organized; you track details and follow through without being reminded

 - Clear communicator; can coordinate across teams, collaborators, and vendors

 - Comfortable with spreadsheets, schedules, and project management tools (Notion, Airtable, Google Sheets)

 - Familiar with creative workflows—physical (shoots, events) and digital (video, web, motion, 3D)

 - Calm under pressure; can handle fast timelines and shifting priorities

 - Resourceful problem-solver; figures things out without waiting to be told how

 - Interest in fashion, art, or cultural production—you want to understand how things get made

 - Familiarity with East Asian markets and culture a plus

 - Flexible on hours for shoots, events, and time zone coordination

You will:

 - Support production planning across shoots, events, campaigns, and digital projects

 - Track project timelines, deliverables, and budgets across physical and digital workstreams

 - Coordinate schedules and handoffs across internal teams and external collaborators—photographers, editors, developers, animators, vendors

 - Manage logistics for physical production: equipment, materials, travel, call sheets

 - Manage workflows for digital production: asset delivery, version control, file organization

 - Maintain project documentation and creative asset archives

 - Assist on-site at shoots and events as needed

 - Flag delays, conflicts, or issues before they become problems

 - Help source vendors, freelancers, and production resources

Not for you if:

 - You're disorganized or need a lot of structure imposed on you to stay on top of things

 - You're not interested in the behind-the-scenes of creative work

 - Fast-moving, unpredictable environments stress you out

Us:

 - $18–25/hour based on experience

 - Part-time or full-time, 20–40 hours/week, 3–6 months (potential extension or conversion)

 - On-site in office in Irvine, California

 - Hands-on experience across fashion, digital, campaign, and event production

 - Access to global network of artists, designers, and creative collaborators

source_jobs.title: Creative Producer
source_jobs.description:
Creative Operations Manager Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia Corporation is a design, fashion and creative agency with studios in California and Seoul. We produce fashion, conceptual marketing campaigns, activations, events and cultural projects—often across all of them at once. We collaborate with underground artists, experimental musicians in both US and Asia frequently.

We're looking for someone to own creative operations across our projects—physical and digital. You'll run production for shoots, events, campaigns, and digital work end-to-end: building timelines, managing budgets, coordinating teams and vendors, and making sure everything delivers on time and at quality.

You:

 - 3–5 years in creative production, operations, or project management—agency, studio, brand, or production company

 - Experience running projects across physical (shoots, events, fabrication) and digital (video, web, motion, 3D)

 - Strong project manager; you build systems, not just follow them

 - Excellent communicator; can wrangle internal teams, external collaborators, and vendors across time zones

 - Fluent in project management tools (Notion, Airtable, Asana, Monday) and comfortable building workflows from scratch

 - Sharp on budgets; can scope, track, and flag issues before they blow up

 - Calm under pressure; thrives in fast-moving environments with competing priorities

 - Resourceful and proactive; you solve problems before they're escalated to you

 - Engaged with contemporary art, fashion, music, or design—you understand the work, not just the logistics

 - Familiarity with East Asian markets and collaborators a plus; Korean, Japanese, or Mandarin a bonus

 - Flexible on hours for shoots, events, and time zone coordination

You will:

 - Lead production planning and execution across shoots, events, campaigns, and digital projects

 - Build and manage project timelines, budgets, and resourcing across workstreams

 - Coordinate internal teams and external collaborators—photographers, directors, editors, developers, animators, fabricators, vendors

 - Own logistics for physical production: locations, equipment, travel, call sheets, on-site execution

 - Own workflows for digital production: asset pipelines, version control, delivery schedules, archiving

 - Manage vendor and freelancer relationships; source new partners as needed

 - Maintain project documentation and creative asset systems

 - Identify operational bottlenecks and build processes to fix them

 - Support leadership with scoping, scheduling, and feasibility on new projects

Not for you if:

 - You're more comfortable executing than owning

 - You need stable, predictable workflows to do your best work

 - You're not interested in the creative side of what we make

Us:

 - Salary + performance bonus

 - Full-time, in-office (OC, California)

 - Ownership of production operations across a wide range of creative projects

 - Work directly with creative leadership and global collaborators

 - Access to global network of artists, designers, and cultural projects

source_jobs.title: Fashion Brand Marketing Intern
source_jobs.description:
Fashion Brand Marketing Intern

Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia Corporation is a design, fashion and creative agency with studios in California and Seoul. We produce fashion, conceptual marketing campaigns, activations, events and cultural projects—often across all of them at once. We collaborate with underground artists, experimental musicians in both US and Asia frequently.

We're looking for a marketing intern to help build and execute brand campaigns for our fashion projects. You'll work across strategy, content, partnerships, and launches—supporting how our work reaches the world.

You:

 - Student or recent graduate in marketing, communications, fashion business, or related field—or 0–2 years in a brand or marketing role

 - Strong writer; can adapt voice across brand copy, emails, and decks

 - Visual sensibility; understands how brand, product, and content work together

 - Familiar with social platforms, digital marketing, and campaign mechanics

 - Interest in fashion PR, partnerships, and cultural positioning

 - Plugged into underground and experimental fashion, art, or music scenes

 - Familiarity with East Asian markets and culture a plus

 - Organized; can manage multiple projects and deadlines

You will:

 - Support planning and execution of product launches and campaigns

 - Draft brand copy, press materials, and partnership outreach

 - Research press contacts, influencers, and potential collaborators

 - Coordinate with internal creative teams on marketing assets

 - Help manage campaign timelines and deliverables

 - Track campaign performance and compile reports

 - Monitor fashion and cultural trends relevant to brand positioning

Not for you if:

 - You're interested in conventional or corporate brand marketing

 - You're not engaged with fashion or cultural trends

 - You need heavy supervision to stay on track

Us:

 - $18–25/hour based on experience

 - Part-time or full-time, 20–40 hours/week, 3–6 months (potential extension or conversion)

 - In-person, in-office (occasional evenings/weekends for events)

 - Work on campaigns for conceptual fashion and cultural projects

 - Access to global network of artists, designers, and brand partners

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant to Creative Director
source_jobs.description:
EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT TO CREATIVE DIRECTOR/CEO

Global Art/Design Studio




ABOUT US

We are an experimental art, design, and fashion studio expanding into social technology. Our practice spans fashion releases, art projects, film production, and underground music events, all pursuing an avant-garde, indie aesthetic. With operations between Orange County and East Asia (Korea, Shanghai, Tokyo), we maintain exceptionally high standards in creative execution and professional presentation.




POSITION OVERVIEW

We seek a sophisticated Executive Assistant to provide comprehensive support to our Creative Director/CEO. This role requires someone who appreciates artistic excellence and can uphold the refined standards of presentation and communication that define our international practice. The ideal candidate will seamlessly manage both professional coordination and personal logistics while maintaining absolute discretion and attention to aesthetic detail.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Professional Support:

 - Serve as primary communication interface for CEO across all channels

 - Coordinate complex scheduling across multiple time zones and international travel

 - Prepare detailed meeting materials and maintain meticulous project documentation

 - Manage relationships with artists, cultural partners, collaborators, and industry contacts

 - Provide remote administrative support during CEO's regular trips to East Asia

 - Represent the company with appropriate sophistication at cultural events and industry functions

Project Coordination:

 - Track progress across multiple creative projects (fashion, art, film, music)

 - Interface between CEO and internal team members

 - Coordinate with external partners, venues, and cultural institutions

 - Assist with event planning and execution logistics

Personal Management:

 - Manage personal calendar, appointments, and household coordination

 - Arrange comprehensive travel itineraries for domestic and international trips

 - Handle personal errands and home management as needed


REQUIREMENTS

Professional Background:

 - 5+ years executive support experience, preferably in arts, design, or cultural institutions

 - Experience with galleries, museums, design firms, or cultural foundations strongly preferred

 - Demonstrated ability to work in high-standards environments with attention to presentation and protocol

 - Native English proficiency; Korean, Mandarin, or Japanese language skills advantageous

Technical & Communication Skills:

 - Exceptional written communication for drafting correspondence on CEO's behalf

 - Experience with project management tools and creative industry workflows

 - Strong copywriting and editing abilities

 - Comfortable with both formal business protocols and creative industry practices

Personal Qualities:

 - Refined aesthetic sensibility appropriate to experimental fashion/art environment

 - Highly diligent with detailed note-taking, task tracking, and follow-through

 - Strategic thinker who anticipates challenges and develops solutions before problems arise

 - Exceptional interpersonal skills with keen awareness of social nuances and relationship dynamics

 - Comfortable working behind-the-scenes to support creative vision

 - Exceptional discretion with confidential artistic and business matters

 - Punctual and reliable with unwavering attention to deadlines

 - Adaptable to non-traditional schedules including evenings and cultural events

 - Calm under pressure with excellent stress management capabilities


WORK ENVIRONMENT

 - Full-time, in-office position based in Orange County

 - Occasional evening/weekend availability for events and international coordination

 - Opportunity to engage with cutting-edge creative projects and international art scene

 - Professional growth within a rapidly expanding creative and technology venture


WHAT WE OFFER

We provide exceptional compensation commensurate with experience, comprehensive benefits, and the

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
FULL STACK ENGINEER
Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia is a multidisciplinary art studio and lifestyle brand building unconventional social applications. We produce underground culture events with emerging artists globally and are building a social media platform to connect these communities.

We're looking for a full stack engineer who moves fast and cares about craft. You'll build social platforms from scratch—React frontends, identity/auth systems, APIs, content management, and database architecture.



You have:

 - 3+ years shipping full-stack applications

 - Strong React and Golang (or Node.js)

 - Experience with SQLite/Postgres, API design, and backend architecture

 - Track record of 0→1 product development

 - Comfortable with version control, testing, and rapid iteration

 - Portfolio of shipped work

Bonus: Swift/Kotlin, DevOps/CI/CD experience



Us:

 - Competitive compensation and benefits

 - Generous PTO, health insurance, lifestyle stipends, Equinox membership

 - Creative studio environment working with emerging artists

 - Access to our global network of culture events

 - Full-time, on-site in Irvine, CA

To apply: include portfolio of apps you’ve shipped.

source_jobs.title: UX/UI Designer
source_jobs.description:
UX/UI DESIGNER

Remilia Corporation


ABOUT REMILIA CORPORATION

Remilia Corporation is a pioneering arts and culture enterprise operating at the intersection of contemporary art, fashion, media, and community development. We're building an experimental social network that fundamentally reimagines digital community interaction, drawing inspiration from visionary systems like Xanadu and Urbit while incorporating elements from social games and MMOs.


ROLE OVERVIEW

We seek a talented UX/UI Designer to contribute to the design of a new social application. Working closely with our engineering team, you'll help create interface designs for a platform that challenges conventional social media paradigms. This role offers an opportunity to learn and grow while working on breakthrough user experiences that have never been attempted at scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


UX/UI DESIGN CONTRIBUTION

 - Contribute to UX/UI development for gamified social applications across web and mobile platforms

 - Support the development and maintenance of design systems under guidance from senior team members

 - Help implement design vision that transcends conventional social media patterns




PRODUCT DESIGN & LEARNING

 - Assist in defining interaction models for social applications with integrated game mechanics

 - Support the creation of information architectures for engaging, multi-platform social experiences

 - Learn to design novel approaches to user engagement, progression, and community building

 - Balance sophisticated gamification concepts with authentic social interaction under mentorship




IMPLEMENTATION & COLLABORATION

 - Create production-ready designs within our lean agile development cycles

 - Work collaboratively with engineering and senior design team members

 - Learn to balance experimental vision with technical constraints and user needs

 - Participate in user research and contribute to data-driven design decisions




REQUIREMENTS


PROFESSIONAL FOUNDATION

 - 3+ years UX/UI design experience with shipped digital products

 - Portfolio demonstrating strong interface design skills and design thinking

 - Experience with at least one successful product launch or major feature release

 - Understanding of design systems and component-based design


TECHNICAL SKILLS

 - Proficiency in professional design tools (Figma, Sketch, Adobe Creative Suite)

 - Understanding of front-end development and mobile-first design principles

 - Familiarity with user research methodologies and usability testing

 - Experience collaborating with developers in agile environments


RELEVANT EXPERIENCE (PREFERRED)

 - Interest in or experience with social platform design, game UX, or digital community experiences

 - Understanding of user engagement, gamification principles, or community dynamics

 - Experience with modern interface patterns and responsive design

 - Interest in experimental or unconventional design approaches


PERSONAL QUALITIES

 - Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new design challenges

 - Strong communication skills and openness to feedback

 - Self-motivated with ability to manage tasks independently

 - Curiosity about innovative approaches to product design and social interaction


IDEAL BACKGROUND

We're looking for candidates who have:

 - Experience with social platforms, gaming interfaces, or community-focused products

 - Interest in alternative social media models or decentralized platforms

 - Background working with creative or culturally-focused products

 - Experience at growing technology companies or startups

 - Passion for experimental design and willingness to challenge conventions


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Opportunity to learn and grow while working on the future of social platform design

 - Mentorship from experienced design leadership and visionary team

 - Creative environment with freedom to explore breakthrough interface design

 - Competitive compensation package with growth potential

 - Opportunity to wo

source_jobs.title: Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Operations Manager

About Remilia Corporation

Remilia Corporation is a pioneering arts and culture enterprise operating at the intersection of contemporary art, fashion, media, and community development. Known for our distinctive aesthetic and innovative approach to cultural production, we create work that spans physical and digital realms. As an early-stage growth startup, we're scaling our operations while maintaining the creative experimentation and cultural specificity that defines our work.

Role Overview

We seek an Operations Manager to serve as the operational backbone of Remilia Corporation, reporting directly to our Chief Business Officer as their right hand in managing day-to-day business operations. You'll own critical operational functions that keep our creative enterprise running smoothly—from financial administration and HR coordination to contract management and office operations. This role demands someone who thrives in the organized chaos of a creative startup, can manage multiple operational workstreams independently, and brings both business acumen and adaptability to a fast-paced environment.

What You'll Own

Financial Operations & Administration

 - Manage relationship with external bookkeeper, serving as primary point of contact for all accounting matters

 - Review and process expense reports, receipts, and financial documentation across teams

 - Coordinate financial transfers, payment processing, and vendor management

 - Assist with budget review and tracking across business units and initiatives

 - Maintain financial records organization and ensure timely completion of accounting cycles

 - Support financial reporting preparation and documentation

HR & People Operations

 - Serve as internal point of contact for all HR matters, coordinating with external HR resources as needed

 - Manage onboarding and offboarding processes for team members and contractors

 - Maintain employee records, documentation, and compliance requirements

 - Coordinate employment agreements, contractor relationships, and team structure updates

 - Support performance management processes and HR policy implementation

 - Handle benefits administration coordination and employee inquiries

Contract & Legal Coordination

 - Review contracts for accuracy, completeness, and alignment with business terms

 - Coordinate contract execution process from drafting through signature

 - Maintain organized contract repository and tracking system

 - Serve as liaison with external legal counsel for routine contract matters

 - Flag contract issues requiring leadership review or legal consultation

 - Ensure timely renewal tracking and contract compliance

Office & Operations Management

 - Oversee day-to-day office operations, supplies, equipment, and vendor relationships

 - Assist with event planning and logistics for company gatherings and external events

 - Manage office space coordination and facilities-related matters

 - Coordinate travel arrangements and logistics for team activities when needed

 - Maintain operational systems and processes that support team efficiency

 - Serve as central coordination point for cross-functional operational needs

Requirements

Experience

 - 4-6 years in business operations, office management, or similar operational roles

 - Proven experience managing financial administration and working with bookkeepers or accounting teams

 - Background in HR coordination, employee onboarding, and people operations

 - History of contract review and coordination with legal resources

 - Experience in startup, creative agency, or fast-paced small business environments

 - Track record of independently managing multiple operational workstreams

Skills

 - Strong financial literacy with experience in expense management, budgeting, and financial coordination

 - Solid understanding of HR fundamentals, employment law basics, and people operations

 - Ability to review contracts with attention to detail and bus

source_jobs.title: Studio Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Studio Assistant Remilia Corporation — Orange County (In-Person)

Remilia Corporation is a design, fashion and creative agency with studios in California and Seoul. We produce fashion, conceptual marketing campaigns, activations, events and cultural projects—often across all of them at once. We collaborate with underground artists, experimental musicians in both US and Asia frequently.

We're looking for a junior assistant to support our CD and Brand Director day-to-day—sitting in on meetings, helping capture notes, and keeping communications and project details organized across a wide network of collaborators and teams. This is a hands-on role with direct exposure to how a creative agency actually operates.

You:

 - Recent graduate in a relevant art field, or 0-2 years in a creative environment

 - Strong writer; clear, concise, adaptable

 - Organized and detail-oriented; you don't let things slip

 - Quick learner; can pick up context fast and ask the right questions

 - High emotional intelligence; reads dynamics, knows when to speak and when to listen

 - Genuine interest in contemporary art, fashion, music, or design

 - Familiarity with East Asian culture a plus; Korean, Japanese, or Mandarin a bonus

 - Flexible on hours for time zones and fast-moving projects

You will:

 - Sit in on meetings, calls, and studio sessions alongside leadership

 - Take detailed notes and help organize them into clear follow-ups

 - Assist with drafting emails and communications—internal and external

 - Help track project timelines, deliverables, and open threads

 - Support communication flow between California and Seoul teams

 - Learn how creative projects move from concept to execution across disciplines

Not for you if:

 - You need a lot of hand-holding or rigid structure

 - You're not interested in the creative industry long-term

 - You don't follow underground or experimental culture

Us:

 - $18–25/hour based on experience

 - Part-time or full-time, 20–40 hours/week, 3–6 months (potential extension or conversion)

 - In-person, in-office (Irvine, California)

 - Direct exposure to leadership and creative operations

 - Growth opportunities in fast-growing, experimental studio

 - Access to global network of artists, designers, and cultural projects

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT MANAGER
Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia is a multidisciplinary art studio and lifestyle brand building unconventional social applications. We produce underground culture events with emerging artists globally and are building a social media platform to connect these communities.

We're looking for a product manager to own the roadmap for our social application in beta. You'll drive product strategy, work closely with design and engineering, and shape how people connect and create online.

You:

 - 3+ years managing digital products at growth scale (25K+ users)

 - Experience with social platforms, gaming, or community-driven apps

 - Strong understanding of engagement systems, social mechanics, and monetization

 - Deep knowledge of online culture and digital native communities

 - Technical enough to collaborate effectively with engineers

 - Track record of shipping products from concept to adoption

You will:

 - Own the complete product roadmap and strategy

 - Write specs and user stories for engineering

 - Conduct user research and analyze feedback

 - Balance user needs, business goals, and technical constraints

 - Drive decisions on community features and engagement systems

 - Work in lean agile cycles with design and engineering teams

Us:

 - Competitive compensation

 - Generous PTO, health insurance, lifestyle stipends, Equinox membership

 - Creative studio environment working with emerging artists

 - Participation in our global culture events

 - Full-time, on-site in Orange County

To apply: Examples of products you've shipped + GitHub/portfolio

source_jobs.title: Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
DESIGN ENGINEER Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia is a multidisciplinary art studio and lifestyle brand building unconventional social applications. We produce underground culture events with emerging artists globally and are building a social media platform to connect these communities.

We're looking for a design engineer who owns the full UX/UI vision and can rapidly prototype concepts in code. You'll shape how our products look and feel, crafting interfaces in Figma, then building functional prototypes that frontend engineering brings to production.

You have:

 - 4+ years blending UX/UI design with frontend prototyping

 - Strong design sensibility and visual taste

 - Expertise in Figma and UX (systems thinking, interaction design, prototyping)

 - Solid React/HTML/CSS/JS chops for building proof-of-concepts

 - Experience designing for social, consumer, or culturally-driven products

 - Portfolio demonstrating original, forward-thinking design work and functional prototypes you've built

Bonus:

 - Motion design and animation (Framer Motion, GSAP, After Effects)

 - Experience with Cursor, Claude Code, or similar AI tooling

 - Native mobile design experience (iOS/Android patterns)

 - Graphic design, art direction, or brand design background

You'll:

 - Own UX/UI design for new features and products end-to-end

 - Build interactive prototypes that communicate intent beyond static mocks

 - Define and evolve our design system and component language

 - Partner with engineers to hand off specs and refine implementation details

 - Explore experimental interfaces, push what social apps can look and feel like

 - Maintain high visual quality bar from concept through shipped product

Us:

 - Competitive compensation and benefits

 - Generous PTO, health insurance, lifestyle stipends, Equinox membership

 - Creative studio environment working with emerging artists

 - Access to our global network of culture events

 - Full-time, on-site at Irvine, CA HQ

To apply: Portfolio showing your design process + examples of prototypes you've built to validate concepts

source_jobs.title: Studio Coordinator / Manager
source_jobs.description:
Remilia Corporation is a design, fashion and creative agency with studios in California and Seoul. We produce fashion, conceptual marketing campaigns, activations, events and cultural projects—often across all of them at once. We collaborate with underground artists, experimental musicians in both US and Asia frequently.

We're looking for someone to shadow our CEO and Creative Director full-time—in every meeting, every studio session, every call. You'll take notes, handle communications, and keep projects moving across a network of collaborators, clients, and internal teams.

You:

 - 1–3 years in a creative environment—agency, studio, production, gallery, publication, brand

 - Sharp writer; clear, adaptable, no fluff

 - Naturally organized; can hold many threads without losing them

 - Detail oriented; take clear, structured notes across all projects

 - High emotional intelligence; reads rooms, adjusts tone

 - Comfortable around artists, experimentalists, strong personalities

 - Engaged with contemporary art, fashion, music, or design culture—you follow what's happening

 - Familiarity with East Asian business culture a plus; Korean, Japanese, or Mandarin a bonus

 - Flexible on hours for time zones and fast-moving projects

You will:

 - Shadow the principal across all meetings, calls, studio time, site visits

 - Take clear notes and translate into actionable follow-ups

 - Draft emails and communications on behalf of leadership—internal, partners, clients, artists

 - Track projects across mediums and collaborators; flag issues early

 - Communicate across California and Seoul teams and creative partners

 - Keep information flowing between leadership, team, and external collaborators

Not for you if:

 - You need structure and predictability

 - You're looking for traditional EA or admin work

 - You're not plugged into experimental or underground creative culture

Us:

 - Salary + Performance bonus

 - Full-time, in-person (Irvine, California)

 - Embedded with leadership across all creative and business operations

 - Access to global network of artists, designers, and cultural projects

We're looking for talented graphic designers with exceptional fundamentals and an experimental approach, dedicated to genuine design craft. You'll work on fashion graphics, event posters, print publications, artbooks, and digital media.

   To apply: Include portfolio and skills

source_jobs.title: Social Media Intern
source_jobs.description:
SOCIAL MEDIA INTERN
Remilia Corporation — Orange County

Remilia is a multidisciplinary art studio and lifestyle brand building unconventional social applications. We build social platforms, fashion lines, digital products, international events, and publications with a distinctive aesthetic and global reach focusing on experimental, underground culture.

We're looking for a social media intern to create content for our fashion and design brand. You'll develop visual content, engage communities, and support product launches while gaining experience in digital fashion and lifestyle marketing.

You:

 - Natural affinity for Remilia's aesthetic and internet culture

 - Experience creating content for fashion, design, or lifestyle brands

 - Strong visual sensibility and understanding of fashion photography and underground culture

 - Basic graphic design skills (Photoshop, Illustrator, Canva, Figma)

 - Video editing experience (CapCut, Premiere Pro, iMovie)

 - Knowledge of TikTok, Instagram, and short-form video trends

 - Understanding of platform algorithms and content optimization

 - Portfolio or personal accounts demonstrating strong aesthetic sensibility

You'll do:

 - Create visually compelling content for fashion and product launches

 - Develop social assets from fashion shoots, events, and creative projects

 - Monitor and engage with communities across platforms

 - Schedule and publish content, maintain content calendars

 - Provide real-time coverage for fashion events and product drops

 - Track engagement metrics and compile analytics reports

 - Research fashion trends and emerging aesthetics

Bonus:

 - Experience with social media management tools (Hootsuite, Buffer, Later)

 - Discord community management experience

 - Background in fashion, streetwear, or design communities

Us:

 - $18-23/hour based on experience

 - Part-time, 15-20 hours/week for 3-6 months (potential extension)

 - In-person, in-office (occasional evenings/weekends for events)

 - Work with cutting-edge fashion and digital products

 - Access to our global network of culture events

notes: Remilia Corporation is a multidisciplinary art studio and lifestyle brand with a mix of creative, technical, and operational roles.
